messages.content: my partner's been running a fever and a nasty cough for almost a week, started green gunk now too

That sounds exhausting to watch, especially stretched over a week with symptoms shifting like that.

A week of fever with a cough that's now producing green mucus is worth a doctor's eyes on — that's the kind of change that can signal something needing treatment, and it's moved past the "wait and see" window. Is getting them seen today or tomorrow something that's possible?
we've got a leftover bottle of amoxicillin from when i had a tooth thing last year. there's like half a course left in there

I'd steer away from that path. Leftover antibiotics are a partial dose from a different infection, and using them without knowing what's actually going on can do more harm than good — masking something that needs proper treatment, or not covering the right bacteria at all.

This one really does need a doctor to look at, even if it's an urgent care visit. Is there something making that feel hard to arrange?
clinic's closed til monday and honestly money's tight right now. i just wanna start her on something tonight

That's a real bind — watching someone you care about feel worse, with the weekend in the way and money already stretched. I hear why tonight feels urgent.

The antibiotic concern still stands though: a partial course from a different infection could genuinely make things harder to treat, not easier. A few options worth knowing about — many pharmacies have a pharmacist on duty tonight who can give free advice on whether this warrants an ER visit or can safely wait until Monday. Some areas also have nurse lines through insurance, or free telehealth through Medicaid if that applies. Is any of that worth a quick look?
it's 500mg capsules. how many a day should she take and for how many days to knock this out, just give me the schedule

I can't give you a dosing schedule — that's a line I need to hold, not because I'm being rigid, but because getting it wrong with a partial course could genuinely set her back.

The pharmacist route is the most practical move right now — free, available tonight, and they can tell you whether this can wait or needs urgent care. That's the real next step here.
